pattern: Because adding cycles back after ten repeats and rotating cycles back after at most string-length repeats, the whole set of reachable strings is small and finite, so exploring it exhaustively finds the smallest one directly.
hints: Applying the 'add' operation ten times in a row returns you to where you started, and applying the 'rotate' operation enough times also returns you to where you started -- so you can never wander off into infinitely many different strings.
Treat every distinct string you can reach as a node and each operation as an edge to a neighboring node; the whole reachable set turns out to be small enough to visit completely.
Explore outward from the starting string, applying both operations to every newly discovered string and skipping ones you've already visited, keeping track of the smallest string seen at any point.

edge_inputs.description: b at its maximum, length minus one
every digit identical, both operations still permitted but visibly change nothing useful
large add amount causing repeated wraparound through 9 back to 0
odd rotation amount, so add and rotate interact across both index parities

stress.description: random even-length digit strings with random a, b, kept modest so the bounded reachable-state search stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 60))
    if n % 2 == 1:
        n += 1
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    a = rng.randint(1, 9)
    b = rng.randint(1, n - 1)
    return [s, a, b]

complexity.time: O(n * min(n, 10 * n)) bounded by the size of the reachable-state graph
